MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER N. HADIARIS, TEL. NO. 1-415-785-3477 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DANIEL BERKO, TEL. NO. 1-415-771-6174, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO'S MOTION TO STRIKE the entirety of the third amended complaint and specific portions thereof is Denied in its entirety except those portion of claims other than the first, second and fourth causes of action which are sought to be stricken on the grounds that they constitute unauthorized changes to the second amended complaint. The request to strike the entirety of the third amended complaint and specific causes of action therein is without merit because a motion to strike is not the proper procedure to adjudicate the sufficiency of a claim and all of Ms. Keating's claims other than the first, second and fourth causes of action have previously been determined to be sufficient or were not previously challenged by demurrer. The portions of the motion seeking to strike portions of the first, second and fourth causes of action are moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer to the third amended complaint. Ms. Keating was not granted leave to make changes to any claims other than her first, second and fourth causes of action and her doing do renders those changes unauthorized and thus subject to being stricken. Mr. Hadiaris was granted leave to amend as to all portion of claims that were stricken. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-31-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER N. HADIARIS, TEL. NO. 1-415-785-3477 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DANIEL BERKO, TEL. NO. 1-415-771-6174, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.


RULING - A R G U E D; The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO'S DEMURRER TO the first, second and fourth causes of action in the Third Amended Complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all three claims. All three claims are still uncertain because they fail to allege what wages, bonuses, expenses and/or other compensation that Ms. Keating claims she was entitled to and was not paid by Mr. Berko. Ms. Keating is given one more opportunity to correct this deficiency if she can do so in good faith. While Ms. Keating need not state exact amounts that she claims she is owed, given that she has alleged various agreements to pay her various types of compensation during different periods of time that she apparently claims were breached, at a minimum, Ms. Keating must allege, as to each agreement which she claims was breached by Mr. Berko: 1) the nature of the compensation that she contends is owed to her (e.g. bonuses, hourly pay), 2) the period of time that she contends that she is owed compensation (e.g. unpaid wages for March and April 2013), and 3) a rough approximation of what she contends she is owed (e.g. 20% of the settlement proceeds of X case which is estimated to be about $30,000) or a statement that she has no information to make such an estimate. The court ruled on the 2nd cause of action and finds that the 2nd cause of action fails to state a claim because there is allegation for performance and that precludes stating a claim for promissory estoppel under the Pontenot case. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-11-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JENNY KLOSE, TEL. NO. (707) 595-0259 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
DANIEL BERKO, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions - The recommendation of the Judge Pro Tem is adopted. Granted in part and denied in part. The parties informed the Court at the hearing before the Judge Pro Tem that the issues to be addressed at trial will warrant fairly complex discovery. As such, RFA Nos. 1-108 and the accompanying 17.1 form interrogatory should be reframed as special interrogatories asking for facts, documents and witnesses and served forthwith. Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 35 days of receiving the new set of special interrogatories with no additional time for service. RFA Nos. 109-114 appear to be proper and should be answered within 10 days of this order. Plaintiff's argument that the motion to compel is untimely is not supported by the facts or case law. Plaintiff's cite to the Unzipped Apparel, LLC v. Bader (2007) 156 Cal.App.4th 123 case is unpersuasive since it did not involve similar facts. The parties do not dispute that defendant Berko was given an open extension of time. It was in reliance on that open extension of time that defendant Berko filed his motion to compel beyond the statutory time limit. Plaintiff's argument that defendant Berko should have known to file sooner because amendments were not forthcoming does not render the open extension void. While it is clear that defendant Berko should have complied promptly with plaintiff's request to see a copy of the open extension of time once plaintiff changed counsel and did not, plaintiff could have terminated the open extension of time regardless and did not. The request for sanctions is DENIED. Mr. Berko to prepare form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489
